intro: Leucanthemopsis longipectinata is a perennial plant species in the daisy family, native to the mountainous regions of China, characterized by its unique, long-petaled flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Leucanthemopsis longipectinata include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Leucanthemopsis longipectinata is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
